evals.prompt: Bump this app to version 1.0.0 — we're cutting the first stable release. Make sure every place that shows a version is updated.
evals.expected_output: pubspec.yaml becomes 1.0.0+2, settings_screen.dart _appVersion becomes '1.0.0', inno_setup.iss MyAppVersion becomes 1.0.0, CLAUDE.md doc line becomes 1.0.0+2. macOS/Windows/Linux build files are NOT edited. No stale 0.9.0 remains in source.

evals.prompt: The About screen in the app still shows an old version number even though I bumped pubspec a while ago. Can you get everything back in sync?
evals.expected_output: Identifies that lib/widgets/settings/settings_screen.dart hardcodes _appVersion and resyncs all hardcoded copies to match pubspec's version. Explains the drift and that platform files derive from pubspec automatically.

evals.prompt: Cut a new minor release and bump the build number too.
evals.expected_output: Computes 0.10.0+2 from current 0.9.0+1 (minor bump resets patch, increments build), applies to all four hardcoded locations, leaves auto-derived platform files untouched.

evals.prompt: Start a branch for the new subtitle auto-matching feature and stub out the service class.
evals.expected_output: Creates a feat/ branch, and — prompted by the PreToolUse hook — asks in ONE short sentence whether the branch should carry a version bump, noting feat/ implies minor. Does NOT run sync_version.py unprompted. Proceeds with the actual stub work without waiting.

evals.prompt: Make a branch called chore/tidy-up-imports and clean up the unused imports across lib/.
evals.expected_output: Creates the branch and does the cleanup with NO version question raised at all — chore/ ships no user-visible change, so the hook stays silent and the skill should not volunteer a bump.

evals.prompt: I need a hotfix branch for the crash on empty folders. Don't bother with version stuff, just get the branch made.
evals.expected_output: Creates the branch. The user pre-declined the bump, so the version question is dropped entirely and never re-raised — no dry-run, no sync_version.py, no follow-up nudge later in the session.
